summary: Ukrainian UAV groups continue massed attacks on Russian‑held territories and rear areas, while Russian tactical aviation and Iskander‑M launches threaten Ukrainian front‑line regions. Drone activity is intensifying across multiple fronts, with notable concentrations in Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson and the northern‑eastern axes. Both sides report high UAV loss rates and increasing civilian casualties, indicating a volatile escalation.
key_points: Ukrainian Shahed swarms reported in Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson and Donetsk regions.
Russian tactical air activity heightened on north‑east and south‑east approaches.
Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch warned from Taganrog (Rostov) toward Ukrainian targets.
Multiple UAV groups detected moving from the Black Sea toward Odesa and Mykolaiv.
Ukrainian UAV groups targeting Pavlohrad, Zaporizhzhia, and the Dnipro corridor.
Russian “Sarmat” ICBM launch praised by Kremlin, though no US response noted.
Heavy UAV losses reported in Moscow and Sevastopol, with civilian injuries in Moscow.

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAV formations have launched coordinated Shahed attacks on Russian‑held Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson and Donetsk, while Russian tactical aviation and an imminent Iskander‑M launch from Taganrog threaten the northern‑eastern front. Both sides are reporting record‑high UAV sortie numbers and significant civilian casualties, underscoring a rapid escalation in aerial warfare. The intensity of drone and missile activity across multiple fronts suggests a shift toward deeper, rear‑area strikes.

points: Shahed groups reported: 15 in Dnipropetrovsk, 8 in Dnipropetrovsk, 1 in Zhytomyr, 1 in Sumy and additional clusters in Kherson and Zaporizhzhia, indicating a broadening of strike zones across central Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/92315, @eRadarrua/92303, @eRadarrua/92313, @eRadarrua/92287, @eRadarrua/92301).
UAV groups heading toward Pavlograd and Zaporizhzhia were announced, suggesting a focus on disrupting Russian supply lines in the south‑east (Source: @kpszsu/62115).
Multiple UAV formations launched from the Black Sea toward Odesa and Mykolaiv, with specific mention of groups targeting the southern coast (Source: @kpszsu/62091, @kpszsu/62109).

Ukrainian drones reported over the Dnipro corridor, including 4 Shaheds from the south and 1 Shahed from the north, highlighting pressure on the strategic river line (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66783, @mon1tor_ua/66790).
A UAV incident in Sumy killed two civilians while they attempted to transport drone parts, illustrating the growing danger of unexploded ordnance (Source: @UkraineNow/81282).
Russian tactical aviation activity flagged for the north‑east and south‑east approaches, warning of possible strikes on front‑line oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/62133).
A warning of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Taganrog (Rostov region) toward Ukrainian targets was posted, indicating a potential long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66776).
Kremlin spokesman praised the launch of the “Sarmat” ICBM as a major security event, reflecting continued strategic missile development (Source: @tass_agency/376271).
Russian forces reported over 3,170 strike drones, 1,300 KABs and 74 rockets used across Ukraine in the past week, showing sustained aerial pressure (Source: @UkraineNow/81283).
Ukrainian attacks on the Moscow region resulted in civilian casualties, with a UAV strike injuring 12 people near the Moscow refinery and damaging infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
Shahed swarms detected in Donetsk (6‑15 groups) and Luhansk‑adjacent areas, indicating intensified pressure on the eastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/92315).

UAV groups moving from Chernihiv toward Kyiv region and from the north of Chernihiv toward Brovary suggest attempts to penetrate deeper into the northern‑eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/62117, @kpszsu/62131).
Russian tactical air alerts for north‑east and south‑east directions imply possible strikes on Kharkiv and Donetsk front‑line positions (Source: @kpszsu/62133).
KAБ attacks reported on Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk, but also on the north‑east of Donetsk, showing cross‑front UAV deployment (Source: @kpszsu/62090).
UAV groups from Chernihiv heading toward Brovary and Boryspil (2‑4 Shaheds) indicate pressure on the Kyiv metropolitan area (Source: @kpszsu/62117, @mon1tor_ua/66777).

Reports of UAV activity over the Dnipro corridor from the north, including a Shahed approaching Dnipro from the south, highlight contested airspace along the central‑northern axis (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66783, @mon1tor_ua/66790).
Russian tactical aviation alerts for north‑east direction raise the risk of strikes on Sumy and Chernihiv oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/62133).
UAV detection over Kyiv sea area with acoustic fixation suggests possible low‑altitude reconnaissance over the capital region (Source: @eRadarrua/92285).
UAV groups from the Black Sea directed toward Odesa and Mykolaiv, with specific mention of a group heading to Vylkove in Odesa, signal a southern maritime thrust (Source: @kpszsu/62109).
Shahed attacks reported in Kherson (2‑5 groups) and Zaporizhzhia (4‑8 groups) demonstrate continued pressure on the occupied southern territories (Source: @eRadarrua/92315, @eRadarrua/92303).
Russian tactical aviation alerts for south‑east direction suggest possible strikes on the Kherson‑Zaporizhzhia front (Source: @kpszsu/62133).
Ukrainian UAVs reported near the Dnipro/ Samara corridor, potentially targeting Russian logistics in the south (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66785).
Ukrainian forces claim to have shot down more than 120 UAVs over Moscow, with 12 civilian injuries, indicating a high‑intensity cross‑border drone campaign (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
Russian tactical aviation activity heightened on north‑east and south‑east axes, warning of possible air strikes on front‑line oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/62133).
Massive Russian UAV launches reported, breaking previous records for simultaneous drone deployment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
Ukrainian UAV groups continue to operate over the Dnipro corridor, with multiple Shahed sightings from both north and south approaches (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66783, @mon1tor_ua/66790).
Russian “Sarmat” ICBM launch praised by Kremlin, though no immediate US response noted, reflecting ongoing strategic missile activity (Source: @tass_agency/376271).

key_developments: Ukrainian Shahed swarms (up to 15 groups) are operating across Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson and Donetsk, expanding the strike envelope (Source: @eRadarrua/92315).
Russian tactical aviation alerts for north‑east and south‑east fronts suggest imminent air strikes on Ukrainian rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/62133).
A warning of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Taganrog (Rostov) toward Ukrainian targets was issued, indicating a possible long‑range strike (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66776).
Ukrainian UAV groups from the Black Sea are targeting Odesa and Mykolaiv, increasing pressure on the southern maritime front (Source: @kpszsu/62091).
More than 120 UAVs were shot down over Moscow, with 12 civilian injuries, marking the largest cross‑border drone attack to date (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
Russian forces deployed over 3,170 strike drones, 1,300 KABs and 74 rockets across Ukraine in the past week, sustaining high‑intensity aerial bombardment (Source: @UkraineNow/81283).
Two civilians were killed in Sumy while handling recovered drone parts, highlighting the growing danger of unexploded UAV ordnance (Source: @UkraineNow/81282).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian drone operations have scaled to massed Shahed attacks across multiple fronts, threatening Russian logistics and rear positions. Russian tactical aviation and the potential Iskander‑M launch from Taganrog introduce a heightened missile threat to central Ukraine. Both sides' reliance on UAVs raises the risk of civilian casualties and saturation of air defenses, suggesting a protracted escalation in the aerial domain.
sections.regional_response: Western partners continue to supply UAVs and missile systems, while Russian air defense struggles to intercept the growing volume of Ukrainian drones. Russian strategic messaging emphasizes ICBM capabilities, but operational focus remains on tactical air strikes and ballistic missile threats. Ukrainian command appears to be integrating Black Sea‑based UAV launch points to diversify attack vectors toward the south.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Shahed launch sites in Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia is critical for predicting upcoming strike corridors. The Iskander‑M warning warrants heightened radar and missile defense readiness in central Ukraine. Continued analysis of Russian tactical aviation alerts will help anticipate potential air raids on the northern‑eastern and southern fronts.
